page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server provides 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AES cryptography, and BMI2. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the n2-highcpu-64 demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU extended instructions. Large-block memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only speeds reaching 211.46 GB/sec and write speeds at 92.34 GB/sec, both scoring in the top tier. Single-core performance, including Geekbench and stress-ng single-core tests, falls within the average range. Database operations perform strongly, as evidenced by high PassMark database scores, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput maintain stable, average-tier performance.

With a resource allocation of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this instance is designed for compute-heavy workloads where memory requirements are minimal. This high compute-to-memory density provides qualitative cost efficiency by avoiding the overhead of unutilized RAM. The server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and moderate-scale LLM inference, such as prompt processing and text generation with 135M and 7B parameter models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, delivering 1.0 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, it features 32 physical cores, hyper-threading, and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and high large-block memory bandwidth, placing it in the top 25% of tested servers, while single-core metrics and web serving throughput remain average. This high compute-to-memory ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound tasks. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-64 is a compute-optimized instance with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, built for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring sixty-four dedicated cores and optimized memory bandwidth for intensive multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM
Strong multi-core CPU and extended instruction benchmark performance
High large-block memory read and write bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Optimized for multi-threaded processing, software compilation, and database operations
